content: Hi, I'm feeling a bit uncertain about my career as a paramedic. I love the work, but I'm not sure if it's the right fit for me long-term.
I understand. Being a paramedic can be a high-stress job, both physically and emotionally. What are some of the things that are making you question whether it's the right fit for you?

I think it's just the emotional toll it takes. I've seen some really traumatic things and it can be hard to shake off the feelings of sadness and frustration. I'm also learning Mandarin, which is taking up a lot of my free time, and I'm not sure if I'll be able to continue working as a paramedic when I move to Brazil.
That makes sense. It's great that you're taking steps to prioritize your mental health and well-being. Learning a new language is a big undertaking, how's that going?

It's going well, thanks for asking! I'm really enjoying it and I feel like it's opening up a lot of new opportunities for me. I've even been able to practice with some of my colleagues, like my friend, Dr. Patel.
That's great to hear! It's always helpful to have a support system when learning a new language. What do you think you might want to do instead of being a paramedic?

I'm not sure, I've been considering a few different options. I've always been interested in mechanical keyboards and I've even thought about starting my own business, maybe importing and selling them in Brazil.
That's an interesting idea! Starting your own business can be a lot of work, but it can also be very rewarding. What kind of experience do you have with mechanical keyboards?

I've just been a hobbyist for years, I love the customization and the community around it. I've even built a few of my own keyboards and I've learned a lot about the different types of switches and keycaps.
That's great to hear! It's always helpful to have a passion project that you can work on in your free time. What kind of goals do you have for your potential business?

I'd love to have it up and running by the time I move to Brazil, maybe even have a small storefront in Rio. I've been looking at a few different locations and I'm excited to see what the future holds.
That sounds like a great goal! Rio is a beautiful city, I'm sure you'll love it there. What kind of challenges do you think you might face when starting your business in a new country?

I'm not sure, I've been trying to research as much as I can, but I'm sure there will be some unexpected challenges. I've been talking to some of my friends who have started businesses in Brazil and they've given me some great advice.
